varieties.name: Arkin
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1960s-1970s. Developed by Morris Arkin in Coral Gables.
varieties.traits: Sweet hybrid; Uniform fruit 10-13cm long, bright yellow to yellow-orange skin and flesh. Small wings. Smooth, waxy surface.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, juicy, firm, crisp texture. Few seeds. High sugar content. Low oxalic acid (80% less than tart varieties).
varieties.yield_notes: Very heavy and consistent cropper. Leading commercial cultivar representing 98% of South Florida acreage.
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical adaptation. Partially self-fertile. Good cold tolerance for mature trees. Well-suited to Brisbane's climate. Keeps and ships well. Available from most Australian nurseries.
varieties.availability: Widely available at Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (NSW/QLD), Fruitopia Nursery (Brisbane), Ross Creek Tropicals, Fruit Tree Cottage, and most Queensland specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Fwang Tung (Kwang Tung)
varieties.traits: Sweet heirloom/traditional variety; Large fruit 13-20cm long. Bright yellow skin with slight green tint. Pale yellow flesh. Beautiful star shape when sliced.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with low acidity, firm, juicy. Few seeds. Excellent dessert quality.
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in Brisbane's warm, humid subtropical climate. Well-adapted to Queensland conditions. Requires well-draining soil and full sun. Good heat tolerance. Introduced to Australia from Thailand.
varieties.availability: Available at specialized tropical fruit nurseries in Queensland including Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery and other subtropical specialists.

varieties.name: Sri Kembangan
varieties.origin: Malaysia, seedling selection from Kembangan region
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety, grows true from seed; Medium to large fruit 13-15cm long. Amber-yellow to orange color when ripe. Elongated shape.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, juicy, firm, refreshing. Few seeds. Tropical cordial-like flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, 2-3 crops per year in warm climates
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for subtropical Brisbane. Heat and humidity tolerant. Seedlings grow true to type. Well-suited to Queensland's summer rainfall pattern. Requires consistent moisture.
varieties.availability: Available at Heaven On Earth Fruit Trees and tropical fruit nurseries throughout Queensland.

varieties.name: Golden Star
varieties.origin: Florida, USA, 1965. Developed at University of Florida, Homestead.
varieties.traits: Tart to sweet hybrid (sweetens when fully ripe); Large, deeply winged fruit. Bright golden yellow skin, very waxy. Decorative appearance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Juicy, crisp. Mildly subacid when harvested early, becomes sweet if allowed to ripen fully on tree.
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile. Bears well and regularly even as isolated tree.

varieties.name: Thai Knight
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Medium-sized fruit with bright orange color. Excellent visual appeal.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, refreshing, juicy. Really good flavor profile.

varieties.name: Maha
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Slightly rounded fruit shape. Medium size. Sweet with low acidity.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Can be picked slightly earlier than some other varieties.
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited to Brisbane's subtropical climate. Major commercial cultivar in Malaysia. Good adaptation to humid conditions. Heat tolerant.
varieties.availability: Available through specialized Queensland nurseries with Malaysian variety collections.

varieties.name: Kembangan
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Sweet, juicy fruit. Almost seedless.
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane's subtropical conditions. Tolerates summer heat and humidity well. Good for home gardens.
varieties.availability: Available at specialty tropical fruit nurseries in Queensland.

varieties.name: Giant Siam
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Very large fruit, impressive size
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate. Heat tolerant. Popular in tropical Queensland gardens.
varieties.availability: Available through Turner's Garden Centre Brisbane and tropical specialists.

varieties.name: B-10
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Common Malaysian commercial variety. Finest quality and flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Produces more fruit when cross-pollinated with another variety (like B-17 or B-2)
varieties.growing_notes: Well-adapted to subtropical Brisbane conditions. Benefits from cross-pollination. Excellent for high-quality fruit production.
varieties.availability: Available from specialty nurseries with Malaysian cultivar collections.

varieties.name: B-17
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Common Malaysian commercial variety. Finest quality and flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Produces more fruit when cross-pollinated with another variety (like B-10 or B-2)
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical climate. Good pollinator compatibility with B-10. High-quality fruit.
varieties.availability: Available from specialty nurseries with Malaysian cultivar collections.

varieties.name: Crystal Honey
varieties.traits: Sweet hybrid; Exceptional sweetness level, 15-18°Brix sugar content

varieties.name: Lara
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Flavorful, quality fruit

varieties.name: Korean Giant
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Very large fruit, heavier than most varieties. Skin transitions from green to yellow as it ripens.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with a hint of tartness
varieties.growing_notes: Adaptable to various soil types. Prefers well-draining conditions. Suitable for Brisbane's subtropical climate.
varieties.availability: Available through specialty Asian variety collections.

varieties.name: Sweet Tart
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Balanced flavor profile. Enhanced disease resistance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Perfect balance of sweetness and tartness
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent adaptability to various growing conditions including Brisbane. Good choice for beginners. Disease resistant.
varieties.availability: Available from modern cultivar collections at specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Kary (Kari, Cary, Cari)
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Moderately flavored, popular grafted variety
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited to subtropical climates. Grown successfully in Hawaii. Good adaptation to Brisbane conditions.
varieties.availability: Available through Hawaiian variety collections and specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Bell
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Popular grafted variety

varieties.name: Newcomb
varieties.traits: Tart variety; Tart flavor, good for processing
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for preserves, pickles, chutneys. Subtropical adaptation. Suitable for Brisbane climate. Good for cooking applications.
varieties.availability: Available from Florida variety collections and specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Star King
varieties.traits: Tart variety; Tart flavor profile
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for subtropical Florida and Brisbane. Good for processing and fresh use when fully ripe.
varieties.availability: Available from Florida variety collections.

varieties.name: Thayer
varieties.traits: Tart variety; Tart acidic fruit
varieties.growing_notes: Commercially grown in Florida. Suitable for Brisbane's subtropical climate. Good for juice and processing.
varieties.availability: Available from Florida cultivar collections.

varieties.name: Hoku
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Hawaiian selection
varieties.growing_notes: Adapted to tropical-subtropical conditions. Suitable for Brisbane climate.
varieties.availability: Available through Hawaiian variety importers.

varieties.name: Kajang
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Moderately flavored
varieties.growing_notes: Grown successfully in both Malaysia and Hawaii. Well-adapted to humid subtropical conditions like Brisbane.
varieties.availability: Available from Malaysian and Hawaiian variety collections.

varieties.name: Wheeler
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Good quality fruit
varieties.growing_notes: Subtropical Florida adaptation. Suitable for Brisbane climate.
varieties.availability: Available from Florida variety collections.

varieties.name: Seedling Varieties
varieties.traits: Variable - mostly tart to semi-sweet; Variable fruit size, usually smaller than grafted varieties. Not true-to-type. Can take 4-6 years to fruit.
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, often lower than grafted varieties

varieties.name: Ma Fueng (Ma fueang)
varieties.origin: Thailand, traditional cultivar grown for centuries
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety, indigenous to region; Major commercial cultivar in Thailand and throughout Southeast Asia. Well-established in tropical regions.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropper with multiple crops per year in tropical climates

varieties.name: Demak
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety, indigenous to Southeast Asia; Major commercial cultivar throughout Indonesia and tropical regions

varieties.name: Fwang Tung (Kwang Tung)
varieties.traits: Sweet heirloom/traditional variety; Large fruit 13-20cm long. Pale yellow. Beautiful star shape.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with low acidity, firm, juicy. Few seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive in tropical climates. Multiple crops per year.
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala tropical monsoon climate. Highly heat tolerant. Thrives in heavy rainfall and humidity. Popular in Southeast Asian tropical regions. Widely adapted to Indian tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Available at Kerala nurseries and South Indian tropical fruit centers.

varieties.name: Yang Tao
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Major commercial cultivar in Taiwan and tropical Asia
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala monsoon climate. Heat and humidity tolerant. Well-adapted to tropical growing. Popular throughout tropical Asia including India.

varieties.name: Maha
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Slightly rounded fruit. Medium size. Sweet with low acidity.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent productivity in tropical climates. Can be picked earlier than some varieties.

varieties.name: Sri Kembangan
varieties.origin: Malaysia, seedling selection from Kembangan region
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety, grows true from seed; Medium to large fruit 13-15cm. Amber-yellow to orange when ripe.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, juicy, firm, refreshing. Few seeds. Tropical cordial flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropper, 2-4 crops per year in tropical climates

varieties.name: Kembangan
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Sweet, juicy. Almost seedless.
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT for Kerala monsoon climate. Excellent heat and humidity tolerance. Thrives in heavy rainfall. Popular in Philippines and throughout tropical Southeast Asia.
varieties.availability: Available from Kerala tropical nurseries.

varieties.name: B-10
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Major Malaysian commercial variety. Finest quality and flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropper when cross-pollinated (plant with B-17 or B-2)

varieties.name: B-17
varieties.traits: Sweet variety; Common Malaysian commercial variety. Finest quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy productivity when cross-pollinated (plant with B-10 or B-2)
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala monsoon climate. Perfect heat and humidity tolerance. Major commercial cultivar in tropical Malaysia. Ideal for Kerala growing conditions.
varieties.availability: Available from Kerala agricultural nurseries and Malaysian imports.

varieties.name: B-2
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety, pollinator type (long-styled); Elongated, narrow fruit. Unique whitish-yellow to pale yellow-white color when ripe.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer. Excellent pollinator for B-10 and B-17
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala climate. Often grafted onto trees with B-10 or B-17 for cross-pollination. Heat and monsoon tolerant. Unique pale coloring.

varieties.name: Crystal Honey
varieties.traits: Sweet hybrid; Exceptional sweetness, 15-18°Brix sugar content

varieties.name: Arkin
varieties.traits: Sweet hybrid; Uniform 10-13cm fruit. Bright yellow to yellow-orange. Small wings.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, juicy, firm. Few seeds. Low oxalic acid.
varieties.yield_notes: Very heavy cropper. Multiple crops in tropical climate.

varieties.name: Thai Knight
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Medium fruit with bright orange color
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala monsoon climate. Perfect heat and humidity tolerance. Indigenous to tropical Southeast Asia. Well-suited to Indian tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Available at Kerala nurseries and South Indian tropical specialists.

varieties.name: Giant Siam
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Very large fruit, impressive size
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT for Kerala tropical monsoon climate. Excellent heat tolerance. Thrives in high humidity and heavy rainfall. Popular throughout tropical Asia.

varieties.name: B-11 (Balimbing)
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Superior cultivar selected for Philippine growing
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in tropical monsoon conditions
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala monsoon climate. Well-adapted to tropical Southeast Asian conditions similar to Kerala. Heat and humidity tolerant. Monsoon resistant.

varieties.name: Dah Pon (Dah Poh)
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Good quality fruit. Ranked highly in flavor tests.

varieties.name: Tean Ma
varieties.traits: Sweet traditional variety; Ranked highly in quality tests. High ascorbic acid content (41mg).

varieties.name: Indian Indigenous Seedling Types
varieties.origin: India, grown from seed for centuries. Introduced by Austronesian traders in ancient times.
varieties.traits: Traditional seedling types - mostly tart/sour; Smaller fruit, variable characteristics. Traditionally most Indian trees are sour-fruited types.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mostly tart/sour, high oxalic acid content
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, often prolific in tropical conditions
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT for Kerala tropical monsoon climate. Indigenous to India for centuries. Excellent monsoon adaptation. Thrives in heavy rainfall and high humidity. Widely grown in hilly areas and tropical regions. Used extensively in traditional Ayurvedic medicine and Kerala cuisine. Cultivated throughout Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Maharashtra, West Bengal.
varieties.availability: Widely available from seeds and local Kerala nurseries. Common in home gardens throughout Kerala.

varieties.name: Kerala Traditional Selections
varieties.origin: Kerala, India - local selections over generations
varieties.traits: Traditional indigenous varieties, both sweet and sour types; Variable fruit characteristics selected for local conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable - sweet to tart depending on selection
varieties.yield_notes: Adapted to local monsoon cycles, multiple crops per year
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECTLY adapted to Kerala/Thiruvalla tropical monsoon climate. Generations of selection for monsoon resistance, heat tolerance, and high humidity adaptation. These local selections have proven performance in Kerala's specific microclimates. Excellent for backyard cultivation. Available as both grafted improved selections and seedlings.
varieties.availability: Available from local Kerala agricultural centers, Kanjirappally tropical fruit farms, Pathanamthitta nurseries, and through farmer networks in Thiruvalla region.
